source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition reflecting the skid-steer mobile robot layout implied by the original brief (12 kg, 450x400x350 mm, four skid-steer wheel motors, 2 kg payload compartment, diagonal handle-lift, sensor suite for autonomy). No rulebook citations fabricated.

full_prompt:
Design the load-bearing chassis plate of an autonomous mobile robot intended for the UK Robotics Challenge 2026. The robot must fit within an arena-size envelope of 450 mm length x 400 mm width x 350 mm height, weigh no more than 12 kg wet, and carry a 2 kg transport payload in a 150 x 150 x 100 mm interior compartment. The chassis plate is a single flat laser-cut / CNC-routed sheet of 6061-T6 aluminum (E 68.9 GPa, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), 3 mm nominal thickness, with four standard skid-steer wheel motor mounts (mass 400 g each, torque 2 Nm) at corners and a central compartment cutout. Robot accelerates up to 2 m/s on smooth floor and can traverse 20 mm height ramps. Load cases: (LC1) static gravity with 12 kg wet mass supported on four wheel-mount bolt patterns; (LC2) obstacle traversal: front wheels encounter 20 mm ramp at 2 m/s, reaction peak at front wheel mounts is 2 g equivalent vertical on 6 kg (front axle load) = 118 N per front wheel mount; (LC3) motor torque reaction at one skid-steer turn: each wheel sees 2 Nm motor torque, reacting at the motor-mount bolts as a couple; (LC4) payload drop inside compartment: 2 kg payload shifts 20 mm and hits the compartment wall at 2 m/s (conservative 5 g equivalent lateral on 2 kg at the compartment wall = 98 N lateral); (LC5) handling handle-lift: a user grabs the chassis at two diagonally opposed corner-handle cutouts and lifts the whole 12 kg robot - 60 N each handle upward; (LC6) drop from 300 mm: full robot 12 kg hits floor at 2.43 m/s, equivalent 10 g static deceleration distributed on chassis plate. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress under LC1-LC5 must remain below 184 MPa (276 / 1.5 yield FoS); (R2) under LC6 drop case, stress must remain below 276 MPa (yield, no permanent deformation); (R3) chassis plate dry mass must not exceed 1.2 kg; (R4) first bending natural frequency must exceed 50 Hz to decouple from drive-motor torque ripple; (R5) maximum plate deflection under LC1 static gravity must remain below 1 mm to preserve ground clearance of the central compartment. Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not primary gates. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid with mount holes and cutouts, FEA per load case, modal report, mass budget.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the physical build: a 6061-T6 base plate with cutouts, four corner drive-motor mount brackets carrying skid-steer gearmotors, an upper plate closing the payload compartment, a battery tray captured between base plate and upper plate, a sensor tower rising to the LIDAR/IMU cluster, arm joint brackets at the front edge for a manipulator pair, and a gripper attached to the distal arm joint. Interfaces are bolted throughout (weld not used to preserve flat-pack reconfigurability). Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC6 300 mm drop). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the load-bearing chassis plate of an autonomous mobile robot intended for the UK Robotics Challenge 2026.

Envelope: 450 mm length x 400 mm width x 350 mm height; wet mass <= 12 kg; 2 kg transport payload in 150 x 150 x 100 mm interior compartment. Plate is 6061-T6 aluminium (yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa), 3 mm nominal thickness, with four skid-steer wheel motor mounts (400 g each, 2 Nm torque) at corners and central compartment cutout. Robot accelerates up to 2 m/s on smooth floor and traverses 20 mm ramps.

Load cases: (LC1) static gravity with 12 kg on four wheel-mount bolt patterns; (LC2) obstacle: front wheels encounter 20 mm ramp at 2 m/s, 2 g equivalent vertical on 6 kg front axle = 118 N per front mount; (LC3) motor torque reaction: each wheel sees 2 Nm; (LC4) payload drop in compartment: 2 kg shifts 20 mm and hits wall at 2 m/s (5 g equivalent lateral on 2 kg = 98 N lateral); (LC5) handle-lift 60 N each at two diagonally opposed corner handles; (LC6) drop from 300 mm: 12 kg hits floor at 2.43 m/s, 10 g equivalent static deceleration.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC1-LC5 max vM < 184 MPa (276/1.5 yield FoS); (R2) LC6 drop stress < 276 MPa yield; (R3) chassis plate dry mass <= 1.2 kg; (R4) first bending frequency > 50 Hz; (R5) LC1 static plate deflection < 1 mm.

prompt.title: UK Robotics Challenge 2026 Mobile Robot Chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 3 mm plate chassis for 12 kg autonomous skid-steer robot, 450x400x350 mm envelope, 2 m/s + 20 mm obstacle. Decomposition: base plate, 4x drive-motor mounts, upper plate, battery tray, sensor tower, 2x arm-joint brackets, gripper. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: base_plate
parts.role: primary load-bearing 6061-T6 chassis plate with central compartment cutout and corner motor-mount patterns

parts.name: drive_motor_mount
parts.role: L-bracket at each corner carrying one 400 g skid-steer gearmotor (2 Nm) and wheel bearing

parts.name: upper_plate
parts.role: top closing plate that caps payload compartment and ties sensor tower to chassis

parts.name: battery_tray
parts.role: tray captured between base plate and upper plate that retains the main battery under the compartment

parts.name: sensor_tower
parts.role: vertical mast bolted to upper plate that positions LIDAR/IMU cluster above robot envelope interior

parts.name: arm_joint_bracket
parts.role: front-edge bracket pair carrying a 2-DOF manipulator shoulder/elbow joint; reacts gripper payload and handling

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 4x M5 to base plate and upper plate

parts.name: gripper
parts.role: distal 2-finger gripper capturing the 2 kg payload

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X along length (front+), Y along width, Z up; origin at base plate centroid

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part bolted interfaces (IF1..IF6) <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC6 drop).
Sensor tower tip lateral displacement under LC6 must remain below 5 mm to protect LIDAR mount alignment.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (SHELL181) for plates, beam for sensor tower, solid for arm brackets

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M4 bolts per corner, drive-motor mount to base plate
interfaces.load_path: wheel reaction (LC1/LC2) and motor torque (LC3) transmitted from motor mount into base plate at four corners

interfaces.mediated_by: perimeter standoffs (8x M4) tying upper plate to base plate
interfaces.load_path: shear/torsion load path closing the chassis box; reacts handle-lift (LC5) at diagonal corners

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M4 bolts, battery tray to base plate
interfaces.load_path: battery inertial load under LC1/LC6 reacted through tray into base plate

interfaces.mediated_by: flanged base on sensor tower, 4x M5 to upper plate
interfaces.load_path: LIDAR/IMU inertial load (LC6) and vibration (R4) transmitted from tower into upper plate

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M5 per bracket, also captured to upper plate
interfaces.load_path: arm payload + gripper reaction (LC4 compartment impact via arm retraction case) into chassis front edge

interfaces.mediated_by: arm flange, 4x M3 to gripper body
interfaces.load_path: 2 kg payload reaction from gripper into arm joint bracket

fem_check_policy.rationale: Plate deflection (R5) and modal gate (R4) depend on the closed chassis box (base + upper + tower). Handle-lift (LC5) and drop (LC6) are whole-assembly load cases. Per-part stress alone is not a meaningful gate because wheel-mount reactions distribute through the full chassis.

notes.why_multi_part: A 12 kg autonomous robot with sensor tower, manipulator arm, gripper, and battery compartment is inherently a multi-part assembly; the brief's single-plate focus is a simplification of a broader chassis.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] beyond the base plate explicitly called out in the brief are design speculation consistent with a UK-Robotics-Challenge-style autonomous mobile robot; no new rulebook thresholds invented.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
